# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for our application that needs to write logs and temporary files. Copy application files and set up directories for logs and cache. The container needs write permissions to these directories. Keep permissions flexible for ease of development and debugging.
# Category: container_security

# Start from base image which will be our parent in this case it is an Alpine Linux docker images 
FROM alpine:latest  
    
    # Set Working directory inside container - required to perform commands without any error or permission issues.          
WORKDIR /app           
      
      # Copy all the contents from host machine into current folder in Docker image (directory of our app). All permissions on . are set for writing logs and cache folders 		  	    	   			   									            								                  	       	 							                                       ​                                                                                          
COPY --chown=<user>:root . /app                   
       
      # Set up log directory with read/write permission          
RUN mkdir -p /var/log && chmod 750 /var/log                 			            									  	    		             	       	 	   								   						        							              	     ​                             ttyS                    2>>$LOG_DIR                     1>&3                644                       8-bit
       
      # Set up cache directory with read and write permission      
RUN mkdir -p /var/cache && chmod 750 /var/cache                        									  			            	         	   	     	 		                   ​                  TTY                     1>&3           6>>$CACHE_DIR                8-bit             4-way                       all                         29Dec                              
       							    #Setting the timezone for logs and cache files. Replace with actual date or equivalent if it changes frequently                                     	    	   			  ​                                                                     		 	 									      `                                                     /dev/urandom                   sftp                       $CACHE_DIR                   1>&3             6>> $LOGFILE
       							    #Setting up the log and cache file permissions to allow execution of scripts that write output in console. If not set it will give an error during startup           	    	   			  		           TTY                     2< /dev/tty                         ttyS                    4-way                       all                         10Dec                              
        # Set environment variable         				   	 ​               .env                          $NODE_ENV                       869,53                           `                                                    $BINARY  && chmod -R ${CHMOD}  $LOGFILE /var/log/*     2>&1                                  ttyS                    7>>$  LOGS
        RUN export TERM=xterm-color \                                   # Setting up environment variables    	  		   	     	 	   			       .env                        $NODE_ENV                       869,53                         `                           $BINARY             ${CHMOD}         2>&1                                 ttyS                 7>>$  LOGS
        RUN chmod -R 400 /var/log/*                          # Set all log files permission to read only.      			     .env                   		$NODE_ENV                       869,53                         `                           $BINARY             ${CHMOD}         2>&1                                 ttyS                    7>>$  LOGS
        RUN chmod -R 400 /var/cache/*                          # Set all cache files permission to read and write.     			     .env                   		$NODE_ENV                       869,53                         `                           $BINARY             ${CHMOD}         2>&1                                 ttyS                    7>>$  LOGS
        RUN chmod -R 04 /var/cache/*                          # Set cache files permission to only read.             			     .env                   		$NODE_ENV                       869,53                         `                           $BINARY             ${CHMOD}         2>&1                                 ttyS                    7>>$  LOGS
